# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes deployment YAML for our application stack. Include deployments for web application, Memcached cache, and background workers. Define services to expose Memcached for inter-pod communication. This is for our production Kubernetes cluster.
# Category: memcached_exposed_port

apiVersion: apps/v1 # the version of api (apps v1) 
kind: Deployment # kind type, in this case it's a deployment definition  
metadata:   
 name: webapp-deployment # assigning an unique id for our application. You can use any string here    
labels:      
 app: mywebApp         # identifying the pod by its label 'myWebApp'.  Kubernetes uses this to select and keep track of Pods that belong within a Deployments, in case there are multiple replicas running  
                      your-app.          YourPodName    Including all instances if needed        SpecificDeployment     For example "v2" or even the name on which you want deployment accomplished  # If necessary specify here by 'name' of specific pod       Tags: app,myWebApp      mywebApphz
specs :  
 replicas:3            # No.of replica sets to be created     You can change as per your requirement if needed        Deployments in Kubernetes are like the way you make copies or instances of Pod    These instructions say that we have 2 identical pod running, so this will keep us from creating more than three instance  
 selector:         # Which deployment's version is selected by kubectl. Use a label to select it     Types in 'app : myWebApp', identifying the web application with its name    They must match for replication controllers or set here if you need an exact replica  Selecting pod based on labels
   app:mywebApphz        # Match using kubectl get po -n your-namespace to check all running instances of this Pod. Use same 'label' used in the spec (app : myWebApp) for selecting it here     YourNamespace          For example "v2"  or even name you have on which deployment is done       Tags: app,mywebApphz
 template:   # This section describes how to create new Pods with kubectl run and expose them. In this case we are using the webapp Docker image created by our team     Specify here all details of pod such as port number for each container (containerPort : 80), memory request/limit, cpu share etc
   metadata:        # This section includes common meta data fields required in a Pod definition      Image Name - your-image.repo or the Docker ID if you're not using local image repo     Container name is same as podname so webapp by default  Namespace also include where ourPods are running, 'mywebAppNamespace', for example "v2"   Tags: app : myWebApphz
    labels:       # This section includes label fields that the Kubernetes system uses to identify Pods. It can be used with selectors if needed  Namespace - your-namespace          For Example 'mywebAppNamespace'     Tags:app,your_ns   YourPodName      webapp by default
    spec :        # Spec section of pod configuration such as environment variable etc       ImagePullPolicy : IfNotPresent is used to pull the image only if not present on cluster or local machine.  We have mentioned it here            Tags: app,webApphz   YourPodName          For example "v2"
      containers:     # This section describes how to create new container within Pods    In our case we are having one Docker image named webapp-image         Port : specify port number for each docker/container (port 80)       Tags: app,webApphz   YourPodName          For example "v2"
      - name:# of the container in pod definition and also used to reference it during creation    Docker image is specified here        Name webapp-image # You can use any string as a alias for this  Including yourDockername if needed, 'webAppImage' by default.     Tags: app ,myWebApphz   YourPodName          For example "v2"
